vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #51

plan.title: Speedometer sticks at low speeds while accelerating

narratives.Description: Speedometer sticks at low speeds while accelerating
narratives.Overview:
## Report
On a ride, the speed display **stuck at low speeds while accelerating** — held at ~2 MPH longer than it should while pulling away, and in other cases stuck at ~8–9 MPH while clearly accelerating. It stayed wrong long enough to lose confidence in the display.

## Diagnosis
The view model applies the engine result directly with no smoothing, so a "stuck" reading means low-speed samples are snapped to zero, held, or lagged.

1. **Stationary gate too aggressive in the low range.** `TripComputerEngine` snaps resolved speed to 0 when the per-update position delta is under `stationaryDistanceMeters` (1.0 m) AND GPS Doppler is under `stationaryCreepSpeedMps` (2.5 m/s ≈ 5.6 mph). At ~1 Hz this suppresses real speeds in the ~1–5 mph range, so accelerating away from a stop feels stuck low.
2. **Doppler validity/lag.** CoreLocation `speed` is -1 (invalid) right as movement resumes and in marginal reception; the engine then falls back to coarse, laggy position-derived speed. When Doppler is valid but lags during hard acceleration, the display follows the lagging value — nothing prefers the faster of derived vs. Doppler while accelerating.
3. **Holds extend it.** Duplicate/out-of-order fixes (`delta <= 0`) and the accuracy guard return state unchanged, holding the last value instead of recovering from a better signal.

## Fix
- Loosen the stationary snap so it only triggers when genuinely stopped (smaller distance threshold; only snap to 0 when Doppler is ~0 or invalid, not up to 5.6 mph).
- During acceleration, use the more responsive of Doppler vs. position-derived speed when both are plausible.
- On a valid-Doppler duplicate fix, still update the displayed speed instead of only holding.
- Add unit tests: accelerate-from-stop ramp, invalid-Doppler ramp.

references.title: Issue #51: Speedometer sticks at low speeds while accelerating
